title: Tapping to Work With the Body, Not Against It
subtitle: A tapping practice for learning to honour energy and rest cycles
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 23 of a 30-day sleep program. The script addresses the habit of overriding the body's signals — pushing through tiredness, ignoring the afternoon dip, running on pressure alone. The reframe builds cooperation: moving when energy rises, pausing when it dips.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 23 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use during the day when you notice you have been pushing through tiredness, or when rest feels like something that needs to be earned before you are allowed it.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 23 of a 30-day sleep program. The script addresses the habit of overriding the body's energy and rest signals — pushing through when tired, treating rest as a reward rather than a rhythm. The reframe builds cooperation: moving when energy rises, pausing when it dips.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I push through when I'm tired, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I override my signals, I honour the part of me that wants to stay productive.
Even though I've ignored my body's rhythm, I'm open to learning how to cooperate with it again.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: My body talks to me all day long.
Side of eye: It asks for rest and I keep saying later.
Under eye: I forget that energy has seasons.
Under nose: I can't expect summer in my body all year round.
Chin: Rest doesn't stop progress — it sustains it.
Collarbone: I can work with my natural waves instead of fighting them.
Under arm: My energy grows when I respect it.
Top of head: Balance is smarter than push.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I move when energy rises and pause when it dips.
Side of eye: My body and I are on the same team.
Under eye: I'm building trust one signal at a time.
Under nose: Listening makes life smoother.
Chin: I rest without guilt and act without pressure.
Collarbone: I'm learning the rhythm that works for me.
Under arm: My body feels safe being heard.
Top of head: I flow with life, not against it.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 23 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
